BARBARA A. LESSER
MOY A LESSER
A PRoEES5IONAI CORPORAION
2397 SHATTUCK AVENUE, SUITE 215
BERKELEY, caL,FoRN,a 
Jtn I
TEL. (415) A4A- 0630
:J 
rFAx 
(4rs) 644 - 0636
June 6, 1991
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attention: I-egal Division
Re: ABAG Finance Authority for Non-Profit Corporations
To Whom it May Concern:
The above-referenced joint powers agency hereby requests from you an internal
advice letter stating whether or not it is required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code
pursuant to the Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 81000, et seq.
The Authority acts as a conduit issuer on behalf of non-profit corporations which need
financing for real or personal property acquisitions which serve public purposes. To date the
Authority has issued certificates of participation in the total sum of $18,500,000 on behalf of
two retirement facilities. It anticipates issuing approximately $15,500,000 additional
certificates of participation in the next several months on behalf of the United way of Santa
Clara County and the San Francisco YMCA. Title to real property purchased or
improvements to real property financed through issuances by the Authority has either been
retained by the non-profit corporation or transferred to the Authority for the duration of the
financing.
As of this date, all financings and proposed financings have been insured or secured
by a guarantee or letter of credit and have been in the form of installment sale and
installment purchase agreements whereby the Authority purchases the land or real property
from the non-profit corporation and sells it back to the non-profit corporation which is
authorized to act as the Authority's agent. All of the Authority's rights and obligations are
simultaneously transferred to a trustee pursuant to a trust agreement. The non-profit
corporation makes installment payments which are used to pay principal and interest as due
to certificate holders as well as all administrative costs of the financing. The Authority itself
makes no payments, and has no obligation to make payments even if the non-profit
corporation defaults, but does receive a fee for services. Further, the Authority is
indemnified by the non-profit corporation for any costs, including legal fees, which may be
incurred pursuant to the financing and/or the construction and/or acquisition of the project(s)
involved.


Fair Political Practices Commission
June 6, 1991
Page 2
I have enclosed a copy of the Joint Powers Agreement and Bylaws as well as copies
of all minutes to date for meetings of the Board of Directors. Should you need further
information or have any questions concerning the enclosed, please do not hesitate to contact
either Dari Barzel at ABAG, the administrative agency for the Authority, (415-464-7932) or
me.
Very truly yours,
Bo,^\*qnB$r%
Barbara A. Lesser
BAL:cd:fppc.npc
cc: Dari Barzel
Encl.

I have enclosed a copy of the Joint Powers Agreement and Bylaws as well as copies

sections.question: The above-referenced joint powers agency hereby requests from you an internal advice letter stating whether or not it is required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code pursuant to the Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 81000, et seq.
sections.question_synthetic: Is the ABAG Finance Authority for Non-Profit Corporations required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the request is embedded in the body of the letter. Document appears duplicated in the provided text. The letter includes detailed factual background about the Authority's financing structure and operations to support the request for advice.

embedding.summary: Barbara A. Lesser, on behalf of the ABAG Finance Authority for Non-Profit Corporations, requests an FPPC advice letter determining whether the joint powers agency must adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act. The letter describes the Authority's role as a conduit issuer for non-profit organizations financing real property acquisitions.
